title: comparison_result
description: Deterministic model-vs-Opus extraction diff. Written by scripts/compare_opus_haiku.py. NEVER produced by an LLM call: the comparison is pure text matching, so this artifact is replay-stable for the same Opus baseline + candidate artifact inputs. Two shapes share this one artifact_type: the legacy two-way Haiku-vs-Opus shape (no comparison_mode key — byte-identical to pre-three-way output) and the three-way Opus/Haiku/Sonnet shape (comparison_mode == 'three_way'). The if/then/else below enforces exactly one shape fail-closed: a two-way artifact MUST NOT carry the three-way keys and vice versa.

comparison_mode.description: Only valid value is 'three_way'. The legacy two-way artifact omits this key entirely (keeping its output byte-identical); any other value is rejected fail-closed.

legacy_eval.description: Phase 2. True when the Haiku artifact lacked an `extraction_config` block, lacked a `prompt_content_hash` seed input, or was at schema_version < 1.4.0. Legacy comparisons are EXCLUDED from per-source variance budget computation in tolerance_budget.json so a pre-Phase-2 run cannot contaminate the calibration data.

tainted_glossary_drift.description: Phase 3 (optional). True when the glossary file's sha256 at extraction load time differs from its sha256 at completion — i.e. the glossary file was mutated mid-run. Mirrored from `extraction_config.tainted_glossary_drift` by `pipeline.governed_pipeline_run` so a budget reader (and the per-source state update hook) can branch on it without re-parsing the extraction artifact's provenance. Tainted comparisons are EXCLUDED from per-source variance budget computation, same lifecycle as `legacy_eval: true`. Omitted when injection was disabled — that path is byte-identical to a pre-Phase-3 run.

haiku_prompt_variant.description: Phase 5 (optional, additive). Echoes the Haiku-side `extraction_config.prompt_variant`, defaulting to `production_haiku` for pre-Phase-5 artifacts. Read by `print_three_way_delta` so the operator-facing readout labels each column with its (prompt, model) variant. Phase 6 extends the enum with `production_haiku_with_cascade_filter` so a comparison against a Stage 2 filtered artifact is labelled distinctly.

sonnet_prompt_variant.description: Phase 5 (optional, additive). Echoes the Sonnet-side `extraction_config.prompt_variant`. Present only on three-way artifacts.

pre_gate_haiku_count.description: Phase 4.A G-GROUND-VERBATIM (additive, optional). Item count across the 14 claim-shaped types on the Haiku side BEFORE the substring grounding gate ran. Comparison-artifact readers must treat absence as 'pre-4.A artifact, gate did not run' rather than zero (PR #233 fall-through pattern).

pre_gate_haiku_f1.description: Phase 4.A (optional). F1 score against the Opus reference BEFORE the grounding gate ran. Absence is 'pre-4.A artifact'.

post_gate_haiku_count.description: Phase 4.A (optional). Item count AFTER the gate filtered ungrounded items. Equals pre_gate_haiku_count - ungrounded_count.

post_gate_haiku_f1.description: Phase 4.A (optional). F1 score AFTER the gate ran.

grounded_count.description: Phase 4.A (optional). Number of items that cleared the gate.

ungrounded_count.description: Phase 4.A (optional). Number of items the gate rejected as ungrounded. Equals pre_gate_haiku_count - grounded_count.

gate_drop_rate.description: Phase 4.A (optional). ungrounded_count / pre_gate_haiku_count. 0.0 when the gate dropped nothing; close to 1.0 means the model is mostly hallucinating.

legacy_exempt_count.description: Phase 4.A (optional). Items routed through the legacy_grounding_exempt bucket — artifacts at schema_version < 1.5.0 cannot clear the new substring gate (they predate source_chunk_id) and are excluded from gate metrics rather than counted as ungrounded.

recall_collapse_warning.description: Phase 4.A (optional). True when post_gate_haiku_recall < 0.50 — the gate dropped so many true positives that recall halved. Surface this prominently in the workflow step summary; it usually means the prompt's verbatim instruction is broken (model paraphrasing) or the chunk index is out of sync with the transcript.

pre_cascade_haiku_count.description: Phase 4.C (optional). Item count BEFORE the cascade filter ran. Equals post_gate_haiku_count when both gate and cascade ran for the same run_id; equals total_haiku_items when only the cascade ran. Absence is 'pre-4.C artifact, cascade did not run'.

pre_cascade_haiku_f1.description: Phase 4.C (optional). F1 score against Opus BEFORE the cascade filter ran.

post_cascade_haiku_count.description: Phase 4.C (optional). Item count AFTER the cascade kept/modified items. Equals pre_cascade_haiku_count - cascade_dropped_count.

post_cascade_haiku_f1.description: Phase 4.C (optional). F1 score AFTER the cascade ran.

cascade_kept_count.description: Phase 4.C (optional). Items the cascade kept verbatim.

cascade_dropped_count.description: Phase 4.C (optional). Items the cascade dropped (sonnet_drop, sonnet_response_invalid, modify_broke_grounding, or max_batches_exceeded).

cascade_modified_count.description: Phase 4.C (optional). Items the cascade modified (source_quote tightened to a narrower verbatim substring).

cascade_drop_rate.description: Phase 4.C (optional). cascade_dropped_count / pre_cascade_haiku_count. 0.0 when nothing dropped; > 0.5 surfaces the cascade_recall_collapse_warning.

cascade_recall_collapse_warning.description: Phase 4.C (optional). True when post_cascade_haiku_recall < 0.50 — the cascade is dropping signal too aggressively. Surface in the workflow step summary; usually means the per-type disqualifiers in the prompt are too strict or the cascade is over-trusting drop decisions.
